posts.content:
Everyone's talking about ralph loops.

But most of you are still doing tasks manually.

The gap between people who automate and people who don't is about to become a canyon.

posts.content:
The "permanent underclass" of developers isn't real.

The ones who can't adapt to AI tools will struggle.

The ones who use AI as a multiplier will print.

Same as every other technology shift.
posts.notes: Addresses permanent underclass meme with balanced take.

posts.content:
Unpopular opinion: Most indie hackers fail because they're building products nobody wants.

Not because of:
- Bad marketing
- Wrong tech stack
- Lack of funding

Just... nobody wants it.
posts.notes: Contrarian take that will generate discussion.

posts.content:
2024: "I'll never pay for AI"

2025: "$20/mo is reasonable"

2026: "$200/mo claude code max is a steal"
posts.notes: Evolution meme format. No image needed.
